steps.title: Identify the Core Objection
steps.content: Text 1's decisive signal: genre categorization itself is questionable, not just difficult to automate. The author argues genre categories are fundamentally flawed—ambiguous, subjective, simplistic—and cites Chen and Nair's argument for using other concepts instead.

steps.title: Match the Signal
steps.content:
Text 1's author would assert that genre classifications may not be the most helpful way to think about music—B's exact claim.

The other three choices either assume the author endorses the technical advancement (A), or address unrelated topics: recognition difficulty (C) and user preference (D).

B is correct.
